title: Anduril raises $5B, doubles valuation to $61B
summary: After achieving $2.2 billion in revenue in 2025, the defense tech startup has raised another massive round, led by Thrive and a16z, it says.

title: Introducing the 6 stages at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 — built for today’s tougher startup market
summary: From October 13-15, TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 will feature 200+ sessions across six stages, led by 250+ tech leaders shaping the industry today. Register now to save up to $410, plus 50% off a second pass.

title: Kevin Hartz’s A* just closed its third fund with $450M
summary: The firm takes a generalist approach, backing companies across categories such as AI applications, fintech, healthcare, and security. The average check size for this fund will be between $3 million and $5 million, with the aim to back at least 30 startups.

title: Riding an AI rally, Robinhood preps second retail venture IPO
summary: Robinhood files confidentially for its second venture fund, this time targeting growth and early-stage startups.

title: Korea’s biggest manufacturers back Config, the TSMC of robot data
summary: Instead of building robots themselves, the team is focused on a simpler goal — providing data that robots need to learn and operate. They believe that better data will be key to making robots more useful.

title: Get ready for the whisper-filled office of the future
summary: How will work setups change if we spend more and more time talking to our computers?

title: Voice AI in India is hard — Wispr Flow is betting on it anyway
summary: Wispr Flow says growth accelerated in India after its Hinglish rollout, even as voice AI products continue to face challenges.

title: Fintech startup Parker files for bankruptcy
summary: Parker, a well-funded startup offering corporate credit cards and banking services, has filed for bankruptcy and is widely reported to have shut down.

title: Nvidia has already committed $40B to equity AI deals this year
summary: Nvidia continues to be a big investor in the AI ecosystem.

title: The “people’s airline” and the enterprise AI gold rush
summary: Everyone wants a piece of the enterprise AI pie, and this week, we saw a string of companies making their moves. From Anthropic and OpenAI announcing new joint ventures targeting enterprise AI deployment to SAP dropping $1B on German AI startup Prior Labs, it’s becoming clear that if you’re a startup building enterprise tools, you’re likely an acquisition target.  On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, hosts Kirsten Korosec, Anthony […]

title: Live only at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Why most founders are already behind on raising a Series A in 2027
summary: Learn what it takes to build a Series A in 2027 from top VCs at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, taking place at San Francisco’s Moscone West on October 13-15. Register today to hear this session live on the Builders Stage.

title: Last 24 hours to get 50% off a second pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
summary: Last day to buy one pass and get a second one at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Bring a partner, co-founder, or colleague at half off. Register now.

title: Ramp in talks to hit $40B+ valuation, 6 months after reaching $32B
summary: The company is reportedly in talks to raise another $750 million at a pre-money valuation of more than $40 billion. It last raised in November at $32 billion.

title: Kodiak AI raises $100M at a steep discount, sending its stock tumbling 37%
summary: The company made a series of other announcements during earnings, including a new commercial contract, a pilot program in Canada, and a collaboration.

title: Voi founders’ new AI startup Pit has become the latest rising star out of Stockholm
summary: AI startup Pit is led by the co-founders of European scooter giant Voi and backed by a16z, which is leading the startup’s $16 million seed round.

title: Gusto hits $1B revenue, a figure that brings it closer to public markets
summary: Gusto has shared an impressive milestone on its actual revenue, not an ARR number that estimates future income.

title: Could Lovable’s automatic 10% pay raise be the cure for toxic cultures?
summary: Stockholm-based vibe-coding platform Lovable is offering employees an automatic 10% raise as a way to sidestep classic corporate politics.

title: Kalshi doubles valuation in 5 months, hitting $22B
summary: Prediction market startup Kalshi has raised a $1 billion Series F round led by Coatue.

title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27: A shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K
summary: Startup Battlefield 200 applications are open, but only for three more weeks. Apply by May 27 for your shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, $100,000 equity-free, and more opportunities for major scaling impact.

title: Exhibit at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Get in front of 10,000 decision-makers before space runs out
summary: If startup visibility, traction, and real deals matter, you should be on the TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 exhibit floor. Get your 6' exhibit table before your competitor does.

title: Clio’s $500M milestone arrives just as Anthropic ups the ante
summary: Legal tech startups, including Clio, which just hit $500 million in ARR, are seeing massive customer adoption.
